title: Project Orama
subtitle: A short tagline describing what this project does
summary: A 2–3 sentence overview that appears on the Projects listing page. Describe what the project is, what problem it solves, and what you used to build it.
description:
This is the full description shown on the individual project page. You can write as much detail as you like here.

Use a blank line between paragraphs — each blank line creates a new paragraph when the page renders.

Explain your motivation, methodology, what you learned, and what you are most proud of about this project.
highlights: Describe a key result or accomplishment here
List a technology, tool, or technique you applied
Mention impact — users, accuracy improvement, time saved, etc.

title: Your Second Project
subtitle: Another tagline for your second project
summary: Brief overview of your second project shown on the listing page. Keep this concise — two or three sentences.
description:
Full description for the second project. Replace this text with actual details about what you built and why.

Add as many paragraphs as you need. Each paragraph is separated by a blank line.
highlights: Second highlight — a technology or technique
Third highlight — an outcome or result
